our_argument_summary: Our review says WIN-059 is self-falsified by Kill-Shot Test 6 (39.9% error exceeding 30% threshold), NMP deceleration is explained by Siberian flux lobe dynamics (Livermore 2020), the dome's axially symmetric geometry cannot predict sector-specific behavior, and the claim reuses NOAA data across multiple WINs. Verdict: Std Model Explains. Cycle 1 recommendations for major rewrite were largely applied — the detail_evidence now includes Test 6 failure, axial symmetry argument, OPEN-007, and data reuse. However, a critical factual error persists from the original analysis.

relevant_files: raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 61-65 — Domain 3: NMP Drift Rate. Formula: rate=55×exp(-0.08×(year-2015)) km/yr. Falsification: 'Error >50% for 3 consecutive years'
raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 256-258 — Dashboard: NMP Drift Rate, Tolerance: 50.0%, Status: PASS (39.9% error). Note: 'Adaptive tolerance — originally tighter, widened when predictions missed'
raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 293-295 — Our review notes 'TOLERANCE GAMING: NMP Drift Rate uses 50% tolerance (adaptive — was tighter, widened after misses)'
raw-text/02-wins.txt — WIN-059 not individually listed (V51 addition). NMP trajectory mentioned in PROS-002 and WIN-006/007
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Domain 3 in the dome's monitoring uses formula rate=55×exp(-0.08×(year-2015)) km/yr to compute predicted NMP drift rate. The dashboard shows 'adaptive' values for both predicted and observed, with 50% tolerance. The status is PASS at 39.9% error. No Siberian-sector-specific computation exists. The deceleration constant 0.08 is a curve-fit parameter, not derived from dome geometry.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: The prediction formula is computed (time-dependent exponential), not a static hardcoded value. But the formula's parameters (55, 0.08, 2015) are themselves curve-fitted to historical data, not derived from dome geometry. The observed value appears to be adaptive (possibly fetched or computed from recent data). The 50% tolerance was widened from an originally tighter value. WIN-059 specifically (Siberian sector deceleration) has no dedicated monitoring.
data_sources_used: NOAA NP.xy (pole position time series — same source as WIN-006, 007, 036, 040-043)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The tolerance was widened from an originally tighter value to 50% to maintain PASS status. The dome's falsification criterion ('Error >50% for 3 consecutive years') adds a temporal escape hatch — a single year of >50% error is insufficient for falsification. 'Siberian sector' is undefined geographically, allowing retroactive redefinition.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: CRITICAL: Our review (WIN-059 detail_evidence and Kill-Shot Test 6 section) claims the dome's threshold is 30%, but the dome's own dashboard shows 50% tolerance and Status: PASS. The dome's falsification criterion is '>50% for 3 consecutive years.' At 39.9% error, the dome reports this test as PASSING, not failing. Our claim that 39.9% 'exceeds the dome's own 30% acceptability threshold' appears to cite a threshold that does not appear in any version of the dome's extracted text. The 'originally tighter' note in the dome's dashboard suggests the tolerance was lowered from something tighter, but we cannot verify it was 30%. This is a factual error that a dome defender could use to discredit our argument.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: WIN-059 claims Siberian-sector-specific NMP deceleration. Domain 3 in monitor.py monitors NMP drift rate generally (formula: rate=55×exp(-0.08×(year-2015))), but does not decompose by geographic sector, check Siberian-specific deceleration, or validate sector-specific claims. The word 'Siberian' does not appear in the monitoring infrastructure. Tagged 'none' because the specific claim (sector-specific deceleration) has no monitoring, though the broader NMP drift rate does have Domain 3 monitoring. Borderline case — could arguably be 'hardcoded' if we consider Domain 3 as covering WIN-059's general topic, since the dome's prediction formula uses curve-fit parameters rather than fetched values.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: NMP deceleration is well-documented in standard geophysics. The pole accelerated to ~55 km/yr (2005-2015), then decelerated to ~40 km/yr by 2025. Livermore et al. (2020, Nature Geoscience) explain this via Canadian/Siberian flux lobe competition on the core-mantle boundary. The dome renames this 'aetheric vortex threshold behavior' without altering any numerical prediction.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: NMP deceleration documented since ~2019. WMM2020 and WMM2025 both model the reduced drift rate. Chulliat et al. (2020, Earth, Planets and Space 72:27) analyzed NMP speed variability. WIN-059 adopts the known deceleration trend as a 'confirmed prediction' after the fact. No evidence the dome predicted Siberian-sector deceleration before it was observed.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The dome's deceleration formula (rate=55×exp(-0.08×(year-2015))) is a curve fit to observed data, not derived from dome geometry. The dome's field equation H(r)=8537·exp(−r/8619) is axially symmetric with no azimuthal dependence — it cannot predict sector-specific behavior. 'Siberian sector' terminology is borrowed from standard geophysics (Livermore 2020).

holes_found.description: UNSOURCED 30% THRESHOLD: Our detail_evidence states 'Kill-Shot Test 6 records the NMP drift rate prediction failing at 39.9% error, exceeding the dome's own 30% acceptability threshold.' However, the dome's own dashboard (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:257) shows: Tolerance: 50.0%, Status: PASS (39.9% error). The dome's falsification criterion (line 65) is 'Error >50% for 3 consecutive years.' The 30% figure does not appear anywhere in the extracted dome text (checked both V50.6 and current versions). The dome site explicitly notes the tolerance was 'originally tighter, widened when predictions missed,' but we cannot verify the original was 30%. A dome defender reading our review would immediately point out: (1) their dashboard says PASS, (2) their threshold is 50% not 30%, and (3) we appear to have fabricated a lower threshold to make them look bad. This same unsourced 30% figure also appears in the Kill-Shot Test 6 section (sections.json part5), making this a cross-cutting factual error affecting multiple parts of the review.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace the 30% threshold claim with verifiable facts. Two approaches: (A) Cite the actual numbers: 'The dome's NMP prediction shows 39.9% error. Although the dome marks this as PASS by using a 50% tolerance, this tolerance was widened from an originally tighter value after predictions missed (per the dome's own note). The 39.9% error means the dome's predicted drift rate is nearly 40% wrong — even after widening the tolerance to accommodate failure.' (B) If the 30% can be sourced to a specific archived dome page (Wayback Machine, git commit), cite that source explicitly: 'The dome originally used a 30% threshold (archived at [URL]), which was widened to 50% after the prediction missed.' Either approach is stronger than citing an unverifiable threshold.

holes_found.description: DUPLICATE SENTENCE: The detail_evidence contains a word-for-word duplicated sentence: 'The dome's own OPEN-007 concedes that polar shift dynamics have no working dome-native explanation — making any sector-specific prediction built on these dynamics doubly self-undermined.' This sentence appears twice consecutively. This is clearly a copy-paste error from when the Cycle 1 recommendations were applied.
holes_found.recommendation: Remove the duplicate sentence. Keep one instance.

holes_found.description: SELF-FALSIFIED FRAMING CONTRADICTS DOME DASHBOARD: The detail_evidence opens with 'By the dome model's own published falsification criteria, WIN-059 is self-falsified.' But the dome's own dashboard shows this test as PASS (at 50% tolerance). Calling it 'self-falsified' when the dome reports PASS is inaccurate and gives the dome defender an easy rebuttal. The correct framing is tolerance gaming: the dome widened its criteria to avoid showing failure. This is actually a STRONGER argument because it exposes the dome's methodology as ad hoc.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'self-falsified' opening with: 'The dome's NMP drift rate prediction (Kill-Shot Test 6) shows 39.9% error — the dome marks this as PASS only by using a 50% tolerance that was widened from an originally tighter value after predictions missed. Even with this generous tolerance, the dome's predicted drift rate differs substantially from observations. A model whose NMP prediction requires tolerance widening to avoid falsification cannot claim sector-specific NMP deceleration as a confirmed prediction.'

holes_found.description: MONITORING TAG BORDERLINE: WIN-059 is tagged monitoring='none', but Domain 3 in the dome's monitoring infrastructure does track NMP drift rate (the broader category that subsumes WIN-059's claim). The formula rate=55×exp(-0.08×(year-2015)) generates time-dependent predictions, and the dashboard reports adaptive observed values — suggesting some computation or data retrieval occurs. 'None' is defensible for the Siberian-sector-specific claim (which has no monitoring), but a dome defender could argue their Domain 3 covers this WIN. Consider whether 'hardcoded' (formula with curve-fit parameters, no live fetch of actual drift rate) is more accurate.
holes_found.recommendation: If the monitoring tag is updated, 'hardcoded' is more appropriate than 'live_fetch' — the prediction formula uses static curve-fit parameters and no evidence shows the observed drift rate is live-fetched from NOAA. However, 'none' is also defensible since the specific claim (Siberian sector deceleration) has no dedicated monitoring. Either way, add a note in our code analysis discussion.

holes_found.description: SECTION CROSS-REFERENCE VALIDITY: The detail_evidence references 'Section 6.5' for the asymmetric scorecard pattern. Section 6.5 is 'The 4 Refined Predictions — Hidden Falsifications?' which does discuss the pattern of not counting failures. The reference is semantically valid but slightly imprecise — Section 6.5 focuses on 'refined' (failed) predictions specifically, not the broader asymmetric scorecard pattern. Section 6.6 (95.2% accuracy claim) or the Part 6 introduction might be better targets.
holes_found.recommendation: The cross-reference is close enough. No change needed unless a more targeted anchor exists.

stronger_arguments.description: TOLERANCE GAMING is a stronger argument than the unsourced 30% threshold. The dome's own dashboard note ('Adaptive tolerance — originally tighter, widened when predictions missed') is a direct admission that the model failed its own criteria and the response was to loosen the criteria rather than acknowledge failure. This is methodologically devastating: it means the dome's NMP predictions cannot be falsified because any failure triggers a tolerance increase. Combined with the falsification criterion requiring '>50% for 3 consecutive years' (adding a temporal escape hatch), the dome has constructed an unfalsifiable NMP prediction. This argument uses the dome's own words and requires no unsourced threshold claims.
sources: raw-text/08-live-power.txt:257-258 — 'Adaptive tolerance — originally tighter, widened when predictions missed'

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's NMP formula (rate=55×exp(-0.08×(year-2015))) can be directly evaluated: for 2026, it predicts rate = 55×exp(-0.08×11) = 55×0.415 = 22.8 km/yr. The observed rate is approximately 35-40 km/yr. That's a 54-75% overshoot depending on the exact observed value — potentially EXCEEDING even the 50% tolerance. Our Kill-Shot Test 6 section says 'nearly double the dome's predicted <20 km/yr' but the formula actually gives ~22.8 km/yr for 2026, not <20. This numerical analysis could strengthen or correct our argument depending on the exact numbers.

kernel_of_truth.description: The NMP has genuinely decelerated from ~55 km/yr to ~35-40 km/yr since about 2019-2020. The trajectory has been toward the Siberian sector (crossing from the Canadian Arctic into the Eastern Hemisphere). The dome model correctly identifies that the NMP is decelerating and that the trajectory involves the Siberian region. The exponential deceleration formula (rate=55×exp(-0.08×(year-2015))) captures the qualitative shape of the deceleration curve, even if the parameters are curve-fit rather than geometry-derived.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Four problems persist from Cycle 1 (all still valid): (1) Post-hoc retrodiction — NMP deceleration was documented before the dome claimed it. (2) The deceleration formula is a curve fit to data, not derived from dome geometry. (3) The dome's axially symmetric field equation cannot predict sector-specific behavior — 'Siberian' is borrowed terminology. (4) The dome's own monitoring shows 39.9% error, requiring tolerance widening to avoid falsification. Additionally, this Cycle 2 review reveals the dome's actual threshold is 50% (not our cited 30%), meaning the self-falsification argument as currently written is factually incorrect.

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 2 Review — Focus on Cycle 1 Fix Verification and New Issues

**Cycle 1 Fixes Applied:** The major rewrite recommended in Cycle 1 was largely applied. The detail_evidence now includes: (1) Kill-Shot Test 6 failure at 39.9%, (2) axial symmetry argument, (3) Livermore 2020 reference, (4) OPEN-007 concession, (5) data reuse pattern (WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063 group), (6) Section 6.5 asymmetric scorecard cross-reference. The finding field was updated from the generic 'Duplicate of WIN-007' to the more specific 'Dome's own Kill-Shot Test 6 failing at 39.9% error; axially symmetric geometry cannot predict sector-specific behavior.' These are all improvements.

**CRITICAL: The 30% Threshold Problem**

The most serious issue is inherited from the original analysis, not from Cycle 1. Our detail_evidence claims the dome's 'own 30% acceptability threshold' for NMP drift rate. However, from ALL available dome source text:

- Domain 3 falsification criterion: 'Error >50% for 3 consecutive years' (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:65)
- Dashboard tolerance: 50.0% (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:257)
- Dashboard status: PASS (39.9% error)
- Note: 'Adaptive tolerance — originally tighter, widened when predictions missed'

The 30% figure does not appear in any extracted version of the dome site (V50.6 or V51.0). The dome site explicitly shows this test as PASSING. Our review claims it's FAILING against a 30% threshold that we cannot verify exists. This is the exact kind of factual error our review criticizes the dome model for making — and it appears in a prominent argument.

The irony: the TOLERANCE GAMING argument is actually STRONGER than the self-falsification argument. The dome admitting it widened tolerances 'when predictions missed' is devastating — it means the model's methodology is ad hoc and unfalsifiable by design. But this argument requires no unsourced threshold claims.

**Duplicate Sentence Bug:** The OPEN-007 sentence appears twice verbatim — clearly a copy-paste error when patches were applied.

**Verdict Assessment:** 'Std Model Explains' remains correct. The Cycle 1 recommendation to consider 'Misleading' has some merit (tolerance gaming, data reuse, claiming a 'sector-specific' prediction from an axially symmetric model), but the current verdict is defensible given that NMP deceleration is a real phenomenon fully explained by standard geophysics.

**Code Analysis Tags:** All tags from Cycle 1 remain correct. The 'none' tag for monitoring is borderline (Domain 3 monitors NMP drift rate generally) but defensible since the specific Siberian-sector claim has no dedicated monitoring. Cycle 1 analysis was thorough.

**Priority:** The 30% threshold issue is the #1 priority. This affects not just WIN-059 but the Kill-Shot Test 6 section (sections.json part5), SESSION-CONTEXT.md, and any derived references. It should be corrected across all instances simultaneously.

citations_verified: https://doi.org/10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9 — Livermore et al. 2020, Nature Geoscience. DOI present in detail_evidence. Verified in Cycle 1. Claims about Canadian/Siberian flux lobe competition are consistent with paper's findings.
https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/world-magnetic-model — WMM2025 reference. URL correct and relevant to NMP modeling.
citations_unchecked: The '30% acceptability threshold' — cited as 'the dome's own' but not traceable to any extracted dome text. This is the critical unsourced claim identified above.

OPEN-007 reference — our text claims 'dome's own OPEN-007 concedes that polar shift dynamics have no working dome-native explanation.' Not verified against the dome's Open Problems page in this review. If the dome site has changed its OPEN-007 text, this reference may be stale.
